items.text:
Subject: Confirmation of Your Writing Center Appointment

Dear Marcus,

Thank you for booking a session with the Riverton Writing Center. This email confirms your appointment with tutor Ms. Delgado on Thursday, March 12, from 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM in Halston Library, Room 118.

Please keep the following in mind:
- Bring a printed copy of your draft and the assignment instructions.
- Arrive at least five minutes early to check in at the front desk.
- If you must cancel, notify us at least 24 hours in advance to avoid a $10.00 late-cancellation fee.

Our tutors will help you revise your argument and refine your citations, but they will not proofread the entire paper for you. Sessions are limited to one hour, so please come prepared with specific questions.

If you need to reschedule, reply to this message or call the front desk at 555-0182 before Wednesday evening.

Best regards,
Ms. Delgado
Riverton Writing Center

format_metadata.subject: Confirmation of Your Writing Center Appointment

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Marcus do to avoid the fee?

options.A: To arrive at least five minutes early for check-in
options.B: To cancel the appointment at least 24 hours ahead
options.C: To bring a printed copy of the assignment instructions
options.D: To reply to the message before Wednesday evening

questions.explanation: The email states that if Marcus must cancel, he should notify the center at least 24 hours in advance to avoid a $10.00 late-cancellation fee. Arriving early, bringing a printed draft, and replying before Wednesday are all mentioned, but none of them is the action tied to avoiding the fee.

questions.stem: The word "refine" is closest in meaning to

options.A: To make more polished and precise
options.B: To make shorter and less formal

questions.explanation: In the sentence, the tutors help the student "revise your argument and refine your citations," so "refine" means to make the citations more polished and precise. Making them shorter, organizing by date, or copying from a source do not fit the sense of improving the citations.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the sessions at the Writing Center?

options.A: They will proofread the whole paper for the student
options.B: They expect students to handle final proofreading themselves
options.C: They can be extended beyond one hour if needed
options.D: They are available without any prior appointment

questions.explanation: The email says tutors will help revise arguments and citations but will not proofread the entire paper, which implies students must do the final proofreading on their own. Option A reverses this rule, while options C and D contradict the one-hour limit and the booking requirement stated in the email.

items.text:
Subject: You're Invited: International Food Night

Dear Members,

The Global Cultures Club is excited to invite you to our annual International Food Night on Friday, April 24, from 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM in the Ashford Student Union ballroom. This popular event brings together students from more than twenty countries to share homemade dishes, music, and traditional performances.

Here's what you need to know:
- Admission is $3.00 at the door, and all proceeds support next year's cultural scholarships.
- Members who volunteer to help set up before 5:00 PM get in free and receive a reserved seat near the stage.
- Please register on the club portal by April 20 so we can plan enough seating.

Last year's event sold out quickly, so we encourage you to sign up early. Come hungry and ready to celebrate the traditions that make our campus so vibrant.

Thanks,
Priya Nair
President, Global Cultures Club

questions.stem: According to the email, what must members do by April 20?

options.A: To pay the three-dollar admission fee at the door
options.B: To arrive before 5:00 PM to help with setup
options.C: To prepare a homemade dish for the event
options.D: To register on the club portal for a seat

questions.explanation: The email asks members to register on the club portal by April 20 so that the club can plan enough seating. Paying at the door, arriving early to help set up, and sharing homemade dishes are mentioned in the email, but only registering is tied to the April 20 deadline.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about members who help set up before 5:00 PM?

options.A: They must pay a higher fee for a reserved seat
options.B: They are required to perform on the stage
options.C: They enjoy benefits that other attendees do not
options.D: They can bring extra guests to the event free

questions.explanation: The email says members who volunteer to help set up before 5:00 PM get in free and receive a reserved seat near the stage, so they enjoy benefits that paying attendees do not. Option A reverses the free-admission benefit, and options B and D introduce conditions the email never mentions.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To collect donations for a campus building project
options.B: To invite members to an annual cultural food event
options.C: To announce the results of a scholarship competition
options.D: To recruit new members for the Global Cultures Club

questions.explanation: The email opens by inviting members to the annual International Food Night and then explains the date, admission, setup benefits, and registration steps. Its overall purpose is to invite members to the event, not to raise building funds, announce scholarship winners, or recruit new members.
